extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE won his third stage after crosswinds split the peloton early, trapping YATES, BILBAO, MAJKA in groups that chased for over 80km.
tactical_patterns: QUICKSTEP and JUMBO-VISMA initiated echelons early on flat coastal stage, forcing 80km chase from GC contenders.
YATES took significantly longer to rejoin main group than FUGLSANG and MAJKA, suggesting weaker team support in crosswinds.
Direction change at 80km mark neutralized chasing efforts, rewarding riders who saved energy over those who bridged early.
Intermediate sprint at Taranto with 56km remaining showed VIVIANI finally engaged for points classification after passive early stages.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: caught in echelons and required longest time to regain contact, exposing team weakness in wind management
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: initially dropped in echelons but rejoined main group quickly through efficient chase work
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: neutral: caught behind but bridged faster than other GC riders, showing better crosswind positioning
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: negative: trapped in echelons despite holding third overall, concerning positioning for GC rider in non-climbing stage
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: avoided all crosswind splits and crashes while in pink, superior race craft for young rider
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: first active intermediate sprint showing intent for points classification after quiet opening stages
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: third win in seven stages despite messy sprint, Groupama team control remains dominant
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: second place again but never threatens DÉMARE in actual sprint execution
